mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered instances, or the signal produces a negative information coefficient across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength that supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or quarterly forward returns are negative in more than 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during an established economic expansion reflects strengthening supply-side capacity that boosts near-term earnings revisions and generates positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power during expansion phases, with 5-day forward returns showing no positive skew or Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation breaching the 2.5% annualized threshold forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following inflation threshold breaches, or the signal yields a positive information coefficient in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumption capacity, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth decouples from equity performance, resulting in negative or flat 60-day forward returns during periods of strong job creation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate robust consumer confidence and durable goods demand, which drives immediate positive repricing of equity assets over a one-month window.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to generate statistically significant positive returns, or housing and retail data diverge without impacting equity prices.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with rising unemployment during a late-cycle environment signals an imminent economic contraction that triggers rapid risk-off selling and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined supply-labor deterioration fails to predict negative 5-day returns, or the signal produces positive alpha during late-cycle phases.
